findings.one_line: Reproducing the pathwise derivative uses the observed-data nuisance map, overlap/positivity, and the band-level same-piece smoothness in `ass:band-regular-piece`; it does not consume the true-model tau-band/margin restrictions routed through `P_tauband`, and `ass:regular-piece-lower`/`ass:regular-piece-upper` add nothing once `ass:band-regular-piece` is assumed.

The L2-consistency derivation switches to foldwise quantities `hat nu_{t,k}`, `hat pi_{1,k}`, `hat theta_{t,k}` and an auxiliary score `bar varphi_{t,k}` that are not pinned down in `def:one-step-estimators`, so the reproduced argument is for an auxiliary re-centered score rather than for a single declared `hat varphi_t^cf` object.

The displayed decomposition and the remainder route close only after introducing those auxiliary foldwise-centered score objects, so the current DAG does not discharge the stated CLT for one unambiguous globally defined estimator `hat_theta_t_os`.
